---
title: "The Support Crisis of 2019"
subtitle: "Volume, Capacity, and the Customer Experience"
author: "Nathan Holtrop"
format: 
  revealjs:
    theme: simple
    transition: slide
    incremental: true 
---

## The Calm Before the Storm
* In early 2019, the support team was in a state of equilibrium.
* **Requests** (incoming) and **Orders** (processed) were balanced.
* Customer wait times averaged under **9 seconds**.

## The Surge (May - August)
* Starting in May, ticket volume skyrocketed.
* By August, requests peaked at **25,284**—a massive increase from January.
* Despite the team reaching a maximum processing capacity of ~20,000 orders, a gap began to widen.

## Visualizing the Capacity Gap
```{json}
// Insert the Layered Area/Line Chart here 
// showing Requests (Area) vs Orders (Line)
